name: Line-of-Sight Detection
description: A perception test that decides whether an AI can currently see a target through field of view, distance, occlusion, and visibility rules.
design_notes: Line-of-sight detection sets the fairness floor for stealth and combat AI. It needs field-of-view angles, occlusion queries, target visibility modifiers, grace periods, and debug visualization because small errors quickly feel like cheating.
design_purpose: Let AI respond to visible targets using rules players can learn.
Support stealth, cover, chase, and ranged targeting decisions.
Provide a tunable perception layer that can feed suspicion, alert, or combat states.
player_fantasy: The player feels that hiding, peeking, darkness, and cover matter because enemies see through understandable rules.

parameters.name: vision_cone_degrees
parameters.description: Horizontal or angular field checked before occlusion queries run.

parameters.name: max_sight_probe_distance_units
parameters.description: Maximum distance for visibility probes before lighting, stance, or camouflage modifiers are applied.

parameters.name: line_of_sight_grace_ms
parameters.description: Grace window after visibility is lost before perception fully clears.

parameters.name: visibility_threshold
parameters.description: Minimum combined visibility score needed to produce a perception_event.

parameters.name: occlusion_probe_count
parameters.description: Number of target sample points used to avoid unfair single-point visibility checks.

edge_cases: The target is partly visible behind cover and only some occlusion probes pass.
Transparent, one-way, destructible, or foliage materials need explicit visibility rules.
Fast-moving targets cross the cone between decision ticks and may need interpolation or grace.
Crouch, darkness, invisibility, or camouflage modifiers stack into visibility_threshold calculations.
common_bugs: Raycasts start inside the AI collider or target collider and report false occlusion.
Sight checks run in render update while AI decisions run in fixed ticks, creating inconsistent detection.
FOV math ignores vertical angle and detects targets above or below through floors.
Debug cones show distance and angle but not the actual occlusion_probe_count results.
balancing_notes: Wide cones and long ranges need strong cover tools or stealth becomes impossible.
Grace windows make detection feel less flickery but can make quick peeks unexpectedly dangerous.
Use different sight profiles for guards, snipers, bosses, and civilians instead of one global setting.
accessibility_notes: Represent detection state with readable UI or enemy posture changes, not only subtle audio stingers.
Avoid color-only visibility feedback; combine meters, icons, text labels, or contrast-safe highlights.

implementation_notes.unity: Use Physics raycasts or shape casts from eye sockets to target probes, cache blackboard_key visibility values per decision tick, and draw gizmos for passed and blocked probes.
implementation_notes.godot: Use PhysicsRayQueryParameters2D/3D or direct space state queries from a named eye point; store last_seen_time and visibility_score for AI state transitions.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use AI Perception where suitable, or custom traces with SightConfig; Blackboard keys should separate HasLOS, LastKnownPosition, and VisibilityScore for debugger clarity.
implementation_notes.web: For grid games, use tile raycasts or shadowcasting; for canvas/3D games, run deterministic occlusion probes at fixed decision intervals and log the reason for detection changes.

relationships.reason: Visibility values can weight sight detection by light level, stance, distance, and motion.
Positive line-of-sight detection can feed target acquisition and chase state.
Top-down camera visibility and AI line of sight can differ unless level occluders and debug views are aligned.

example_games.title: Thief
example_games.note: Uses visibility and light as a key stealth concern.

example_games.title: Dishonored
example_games.note: Uses guard sightlines and alert states for stealth play.

example_games.title: Alien: Isolation
example_games.note: Uses line-of-sight detection as a major monster and android stealth risk.
